methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Mariana Minerals is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Turner Caldwell that has raised $85M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-stage, same-sector private companies (Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy) valued between $110M and $200M, supplemented by a broader comparison universe of eight private firms and one public company (ticker: LNZA) in the climate and energy space.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $85M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yields $170M. Peer-median analysis across the three comparable firms produces a $150M implied valuation, with the sample median at exactly $150M and a range spanning $110M to $200M. Both methods are applicable given comparable stage and sector alignment.

Triangulation of these two approaches supports a valuation range of $128M to $195M, with a median of $160M. Key uncertainties include the reliability of $85M total raised as a valuation proxy in the absence of documented prior round valuations, and variability in the peer set—the $90M spread between floor and ceiling reflects meaningful dispersion in market pricing within this early-stage cohort. Materiality should be assigned to capital efficiency and technical differentiation to narrow this range.
